text: ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Israeli drone activity over Dahieh. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇵🇸 Israeli Army Radio: Around 30 Palestinians breached IDF barriers and entered the Jenin refugee camp – which has been a closed military zone since January 2025, with all residents evacuated. The camp is normally held by reserve forces. A drone identified the breach; six unarmed suspects have been detained. Approximately 20 others remain inside and are still at large. Reinforcements, including two border police units, have been deployed. The incident is ongoing. @wfwitness

🇲🇱 JNIM (Jamaat Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin) and FLA (Front for the Liberation of Azawad) militants have launched a joint offensive on Mali's capital Bamako. Fighting reported in Kati, on the northwestern outskirts of the capital, and in Kidal, where Russia's African Corps maintains a base. An unidentified helicopter has reportedly been shot down. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇷🇴 🇬🇧 🇺🇦 🇷🇺 A number of channels claimed that British aircraft shot down Russian drones in Ukrainian airspace today This claim was based on a press release from the Romanian Ministry of Defense, which, however, did not mention the shooting down of drones. It only stated that British aircraft were given permission to shoot down drones but there was no mention of the drones being shot down. Later the Romanian Ministry of Defense issued another clarifying press release, which explicitly stated that British aircraft were only given orders to shoot down drones if they entered Romanian airspace. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇲🇱🏳️⚫️Footage confirms a joint FLA/JNIM presence in Kidal and Gao, in northern Mali, as well as JNIM presence inside Kati, following the coordinated attack against Malian positions. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇲🇱 ⚫️ More footage showing JNIM units in Kati, on the outskirts of the Malian capital Bamako. @wfwitnesz

Mali’s Army says armed groups launched coordinated attacks on multiple military positions early Saturday, including in the capital, Bamako. Residents and journalists reported heavy gunfire near the international airport and in several towns such as Gao and Kati.

Slovenia’s national broadcaster has announced it will not air Eurovision 2026 and will instead show Palestinian films. The country had already withdrawn from the contest, which will take place May 12–16 in Vienna, due to Israel’s participation.

NEW: Türkiye plans to produce millions of drones nationwide SAHA Chairman & BAYKAR CEO Haluk Bayraktar says Türkiye plans to set up drone production and training centers in all 81 Turkish provinces. The goal is to create a nationwide network capable of rapidly producing millions of drones when needed.

⚡️ 🇺🇸 Newsweek : New images have emerged of what appear to be poor‑quality meals aboard the USS Abraham Lincoln, shared by a serving officer. A sailor’s mother said her son has lost 17 pounds and a care package sent in December has not arrived. The Pentagon has denied any shortages, stating that both the Lincoln and USS Tripoli have more than 30 days of food supplies, and called the reports “fake news”. @wfwitness

Visioner 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is not in Pakistan for nuclear talks, the head of Iran’s national security committee, Ebrahim Azizi, has said. Araghchi is in Islamabad only for discussions on bilateral relations. Azizi, a former IRGC commander, added that nuclear activity “remains one of Iran’s firm red lines”. tweet

Faytuks News RT @shellykittleson : Mine for @TheAtlantic on being snatched off a Baghdad street on March 31, beaten, blindfolded and ziptied, and spending over a week as a hostage in handcuffs and in pain from multiple rib fractures sustained the first day. Alhamdullilah I am out now and healing. And I still love Iraq, dearly. https://www.theatlantic.com/ideas/2026/04/kidnapped-iraq-shelly-kittleson-iran/686896/ tweet

OSINTdefender Major clashes are being reported in Kidal, along with several other regions across the West African nation of Mali, as simultaneous attacks by the Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) and Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) appear to be underway against positions of the Malian Army and Russia’s Africa Corps. tweet

Visioner 🇹🇷 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said Turkey could consider taking part in demining operations in the Strait of Hormuz following a possible peace agreement between Iran and the United States. A technical team from various countries would carry out the work; Turkey views such efforts positively in principle as a humanitarian duty. Fidan added that he believes issues related to Iran’s nuclear programme could be resolved at the next round of talks in Pakistan. tweet

Visioner 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 Iran has executed Erfan Kiani, an Isfahan resident convicted of carrying out alleged intelligence missions for Israel’s Mossad during nationwide demonstrations in January. State broadcaster IRIB said Kiani led a group that destroyed public property, set fires and fired guns. The execution followed Supreme Court approval. Hengaw Organisation for Human Rights condemned the execution, noting it was the eighth of a protest‑related detainee since the outbreak of war. tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is not in Pakistan for nuclear talks, the head of Iran’s national security committee, Ebrahim Azizi, has said. Araghchi is in Islamabad only for discussions on bilateral relations. Azizi, a former IRGC commander, added that nuclear activity “remains one of Iran’s firm red lines”. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 WSJ : Tensions between Iranian hard‑liners and more pragmatic officials are frustrating progress in talks with the United States, with divisions spilling into public view. Ultraconservative lawmakers have criticised lead negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf for putting the nuclear issue on the table, while the IRGC opposes major concessions. Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen or heard since his father’s death at the start of the war; analysts say the absence of a strong final arbiter makes it harder for Tehran to reach consensus. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 NYT : U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has been absent from US‑Iran peace talks, including the upcoming round in Pakistan, as U.S. President Trump has outsourced diplomacy to envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Rubio holds the dual role of secretary of state and national security adviser – the first since Henry Kissinger – and has remained largely at home, travelling less than previous secretaries. Critics say the arrangement leaves the State Department sidelined, while the administration argues it ensures close co‑ordination with the White House. @wfwitness
🇺🇦 🇦🇿 Zelensky came to Azerbaijan 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇸🇦 🇱🇧 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Al-Akhbar : Saudi Arabia has forged a strategic partnership with Lebanese Parliament Speaker Nabih Berri, shifting its approach to stabilise Lebanon ahead of direct negotiations with Israel. Saudi envoy Prince Yazid bin Farhan held intensive meetings with Lebanese Presidents Aoun, Berri and Salam, urging national unity and reviving the troika (three presidencies) as a condition for any agreement. Riyadh is pressing Lebanon not to rush into talks with Israel and to wait for the outcome of the US‑Iran conflict. Saudi Arabia is also coordinating with Iran behind the scenes and insists on protecting the current government, with Berri seen as the sole guarantor of Shia participation and internal stability. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to Рыбарь: "📝О горизонте войны на истощение📝 В интервью Михаила Звинчука с Анатолием Кузичевым прозвучал и вполне конкретный прогноз — тот, о котором в патриотической среде мало говорят. Речь о том, что удары ВСУ давно сместились с фронта на экономический контур страны:…" 📝 Будет ли мобилизация? 📝 Один из самых животрепещущих вопросов, который регулярно задают на стримах и в боте обратной связи, — нужна ли мобилизация и когда она будет. В интервью Михаила Звинчука с Анатолием Кузичевым эту тему, естественно, тоже не обошли стороной. Мнение военного руководства в целом сводится к следующему. Да, мобилизация нужна — но не потому, что людей не хватает в принципе. Поток желающих идти на контракт действительно не иссякает, очереди у военкоматов — это не успокоительные реляции чиновников, а реальная картина. Проблема в другом: в слаженности. За прошлый год до 80% потерь пришлись на так называемых «перворазников» — добровольцев и контрактников, которыми по двое-трое укомплектовывают уже воюющие штурмовые группы и отправляют на задачу практически с колёс. Потери случаются не потому, что сами бойцы плохо подготовлены, а потому, что подразделение вокруг них не слаженно — а слаженность быстро не делается. Чтобы эту проблему решить, с военной точки зрения нужно готовить полноценные новые соединения , которые будут действовать как единый организм. А для этого необходимо единовременное привлечение большого количества людей — отсюда и разговор о цифрах порядка миллиона человек и сроке около года. Это ни в коем случае не призывы к тотальной мобилизации, а скорее объяснение точки зрения военнослужащих — и в первую очередь той простой реалии, что военные потребности не всегда соответствуют политическим. #видео #интервью #Россия #Украина ✈ RU | ✈ EN | ✉ MAX ✉️ VK | ✉️ RuTube | ✉️ ОК | ✉️ Дзен 💸 Поддержать нас

In addition to security cooperation, today we will work on deepening cooperation in energy, economy, and humanitarian areas. Glory to Ukraine! tweet

He claimed that Iran’s missile superiority is now acknowledged by international observers. tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇵🇰 Al-Akhbar: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has arrived in Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials, but Tehran insists no meeting with American officials is planned. Iranian Foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said the visit is for bilateral consultations on ending the “aggressive war imposed by the US and the Zionist entity”. A Pakistani official told ABC that the two delegations will hold separate meetings with Pakistani officials, and if those go well, a direct meeting could take place on Sunday. @wfwitness
Photos said to be from the USS Abraham Lincoln show small meal portions being served to U.S. sailors. Source: Newsweek

⚡️ 🇮🇷 Tehran’s Imam Khomeini International Airport reopened on Saturday morning, with international flights resuming. The first passengers were flown to Medina, Muscat and Istanbul. Iran Air will resume scheduled flights to Baku, Najaf, Baghdad and Doha in the coming days. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iran’s judiciary chief, Gholamhossein Mohseni‑Eje’i, said the anniversary of the U.S.’s "humiliating defeat" in Tabas has a different colour today. He claimed that "enemy commandos" were grounded in Isfahan during the war and suffered a heavy and instructive defeat at the hands of Iran’s armed forces. “The Americans should know that they are also incapable of imposing a naval blockade on Iran. Tabas and Isfahan will be repeated in the waters of the Persian Gulf,” he warned. @wfwitness
Taiwan’s Deputy Foreign Minister Francois Wu: What we are most afraid of is to put Taiwan on the menu of the talk between Xi Jinping and Trump. Source: Bloomberg

⚡️ 🇷🇺 🇷🇴 🇬🇧 🇺🇸 Russian forces launched drone attacks against civilian and infrastructure targets in Ukraine near the Romanian border in Tulcea County early Saturday, prompting two RAF Eurofighter Typhoons to scramble from Borcea Air Base. Romania’s MoD stated the pilots were authorized to engage drones and that multiple explosions were reported near Reni, Ukraine. However, the UK MoD said the Typhoons returned to base without engaging any Russian assets and did not enter Ukrainian airspace. Drone fragments fell in Galați, Romania, damaging an outbuilding and an electricity pole. @wfwitness

The US pressured the UK not to oppose Argentina’s purchase of F-16 fighter jets from Denmark in 2024. Britain was concerned due to tensions over the Falkland Islands, but had no direct veto. Source: The Telegraph

He claimed that Iran’s missile superiority is now acknowledged by international observers. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇸🇴 UKMTO reports that a tanker has been hijacked 45 nautical miles north‑east of Mareeyo, Somalia. Unauthorised persons took control of the vessel and manoeuvred it 77 nautical miles south within Somali territorial waters. The vessel’s last known position was 08°56'N, 050°32'E. @wfwitness

⚡️ ☀️ 🇮🇶 🇮🇷 The Kurdistan Regional Government of Iraq has documented 809 drone and missile attacks on the Kurdistan Region over a 50‑day period (28 February – 20 April 2026), resulting in 20 fatalities and 123 injuries. Drone attacks accounted for 701 of the incidents; missiles for 108. Erbil province was the hardest hit, with 477 attacks, 10 killed and 83 wounded. Despite maintaining neutrality in the US‑Israel‑Iran conflict, the region has suffered spillover damage. @wfwitness
Argentina's President Javier Milei: THE MALVINAS WERE, ARE, AND ALWAYS WILL BE ARGENTINE.

BIG: British Royal Air Force Typhoon jets shot down Russian drones over Ukrainian territory near Reni early Saturday, after scrambling from Romania under NATO air policing. The aircraft engaged the drones while flying in Romanian airspace, targeting them across the border. Explosions were reported near Reni, and debris later fell in parts of Romania.
